event: had melenic stool on rectal examination
admitted to the medical intensive care unit
diffuse lymphadenopathy concerning for metastatic lymphoma
pathology findings consistent with Hodgkin lymphoma
acute renal failure requiring temporary hemodialysis
septic shock requiring vasopressor support with norepinephrine at a dose of 18 μg/min
sedated with continuous infusions of propofol and fentanyl on the ventilator in the ICU
telemetry displayed a sudden onset of ST-segment elevations
no murmurs were detected on cardiac auscultation
oxygen saturation of 99% on mechanical ventilation
ECG following onset of ST-segment elevations on telemetry
interpreted by the computer algorithm as a ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction
bedside transthoracic echocardiogram showed the left ventricular ejection fraction to be 60% to 65%
on placement of the ultrasound probe in the epigastric region, there was abrupt resolution of ST-segment elevations on telemetry
gastric distention and findings consistent with ileus
ST-segment elevations resolved with management and resolution of the underlying gastrointestinal issue
gastric decompression with nasogastric tube placement
ECG demonstrated resolution of ST-segment elevations
development of new-onset seizures and focal neurological deficits
Magnetic resonance imaging of the brain confirmed leptomeningeal enhancement concerning for metastatic spread
placed on comfort care because of his grave prognosis
